Data loading and cleaning - BRFSS

Riccardo Gasparini | Gianluca Michelon | Elisa Pascon | Samuele Pasin

source("BRFSS_ne_data_prep.R")

Taking a glimpse of what the data looks like

glimpse(brfss.design$variables)
## Rows: 47,810
## Columns: 38
## $ X_PSU         <dbl> 2.024e+09, 2.024e+09, 2.024e+09, 2.024e+09, 2.024e+09, 2…

Summary:

brfss.design %>%
  tbl_svysummary(
    include  = c(X_AGE_G, X_SEX, X_EDUCAG, X_INCOMG1, X_HLTHPL2),
    label    = list(
      X_AGE_G   ~ "Age group",
      X_SEX     ~ "Sex",
      X_EDUCAG  ~ "Education",
      X_INCOMG1 ~ "Household income",
      X_HLTHPL2 ~ "Health insurance"
    ),
    statistic = list(all_categorical() ~ "{p}% ({n_unweighted})")
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Weighted demographic characteristics, BRFSS 2024**")
Weighted demographic characteristics, BRFSS 2024
Characteristic N = 12,413,6051
Age group
    Age 18 to 24 12% (2,348)
    Age 25 to 34 16% (4,576)
    Age 35 to 44 16% (5,661)
    Age 45 to 54 15% (6,085)
    Age 55 to 64 17% (9,347)
    Age 65 or older 25% (19,793)
Sex
    Male 49% (22,611)
    Female 51% (25,199)
Education
    1 8.5% (2,081)
    2 26% (10,773)
    3 26% (10,968)
    4 39% (23,722)
    Unknown 80,889
Household income
    <15k 4.9% (1,796)
    15–25k 7.2% (3,185)
    25–35k 8.8% (3,852)
    35–50k 9.9% (4,298)
    50–100k 28% (11,833)
    100–200k 27% (9,537)
    200k+ 14% (3,878)
    Unknown 2,792,776
Health insurance
    Insured 95% (44,237)
    Not insured 4.8% (1,693)
    Unknown 658,628
1 % (n (unweighted))

Demographic features

Socioeconomic features

ggplot(brfss24_sub %>% drop_na(X_INCOMG1)) +
  geom_bar(
    aes(X_INCOMG1, fill = X_INCOMG1),
    col = "black",
    linewidth = 0.2,
    alpha = .8
  ) +
  labs(x = "Income", y = "Count", title = "Income distribution") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlOrRd") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") 

brfss.design %>%
  filter(!is.na(X_INCOMG1)) %>%
  group_by(X_INCOMG1) %>%
  summarise(prop = survey_mean(vartype = "ci")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(X_INCOMG1, prop, fill = X_INCOMG1)) +
  geom_col(col = "black",
           linewidth = 0.2,
           alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Income", y = "Weighted proportion", title = "Income distribution (weighted)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
